safetemp.title: SafeTemp Disposable Cups
safetemp.client_brief: I am developing a disposable hot-beverage cup called SafeTemp™ that has a built-in thermometer strip down the side. The strip is a non-toxic, medical-grade thermochromic indicator that changes color based on the temperature of the liquid inside: green for safe to drink, yellow for caution, red for too hot. The temperature scale on the strip is anchored to the 2008 Brown and Diller study showing the optimal serving range for hot beverages is 125 to 136 degrees Fahrenheit (54 to 60 Celsius), with a preferred value at 136 degrees Fahrenheit (57.8 Celsius). The cup body uses standard food-grade paper materials in small, medium, and large sizes. Primary customers are parents and caregivers buying for their children, where accidental burns are the most acute problem. Secondary customers are coffee shops, cafes, quick-service food chains, schools, daycares, and eldercare facilities that face liability exposure around hot drinks. The product solves the problem that hot beverage temperature cannot be reliably gauged by touch or sight, and current cooling workarounds (testing the drink yourself, blowing on it) are slow or unsanitary. I want to know if this is patentable and where the prior art sits.

cpc_codes.why: This is the primary classification for containers incorporating temperature indication, where most of the blocking prior art lives and where SafeTemp claims would be examined.
cpc_codes.label: Containers with thermal insulation or temperature-indicating means

cpc_codes.why: Covers the thermochromic indicator element itself and its color-change mechanism, relevant to the material composition and calibration claims.
cpc_codes.label: Thermometers using changes in physical or chemical properties of materials, using thermochromic indicators

cpc_codes.why: Addresses the single-use paper cup substrate construction, relevant when combining the indicator element with the food-grade paper body claim.
cpc_codes.label: Containers made of paper or cardboard, rigid or semi-rigid cups

cpc_codes.why: Directly captures the burn-prevention use-case framing, useful for differentiating SafeTemp on intended physiological safety application versus generic temperature display.
cpc_codes.label: Drinking vessels or lids with means for preventing scalding or burning

cpc_codes.why: Relevant if claims are pursued on the specific liquid crystal or leuco-dye indicator composition calibrated to the 54-60 degrees Celsius window.
cpc_codes.label: Liquid crystal compositions for thermochromic applications

prior_art.title: Temperature-indicating sleeve and related container
prior_art.abstract: A sleeve incorporating a temperature indicator visible from outside that conveys the temperature of container contents to the user.
prior_art.relevance: This is the closest structural analog: a temperature-indicating sleeve for a beverage container using exterior-visible indicator, directly overlapping the SafeTemp cup-plus-strip form factor.

prior_art.title: Beverage container having thermal indicator
prior_art.abstract: A thermochromic thermal indicator affixed to the inner wall of a beverage container that senses and signals a specified liquid temperature range.
prior_art.relevance: Establishes prior art for thermochromic material affixed to a beverage container wall specifically to indicate a defined liquid temperature range, hitting the core concept of SafeTemp.

prior_art.title: Temperature-indicating container
prior_art.abstract: A container with a temperature indicator in or on the side wall or bottom, visible from outside, conveying the temperature of its contents.
prior_art.relevance: Covers the side-wall exterior-visible temperature indicator on a container, directly anticipating the strip-on-the-side geometry of SafeTemp.

prior_art.title: Temperature-indicating container
prior_art.abstract: A temperature-indicating container with thermal insulation and a side-wall or bottom temperature indicator visible from outside the container.
prior_art.relevance: International counterpart to the Laguardia US application, extending the prior art coverage globally and reinforcing that the general side-wall indicator concept is well-claimed.

prior_art.title: Advanced multi-element consumable-disposable products
prior_art.abstract: Incorporation of multiple interactive elements including temperature-responsive indicators into high-volume disposable consumable products at minimal incremental manufacturing cost.
prior_art.relevance: Covers the integration of color-change interactive indicators into disposable consumables, which maps onto the SafeTemp paper-cup-plus-thermochromic-strip construction and manufacturing approach.

prior_art.title: Co-topo polymeric compositions, devices and systems for controlling threshold temperature indication
prior_art.abstract: Polymer-based indicator compositions that undergo reversible or irreversible color change at controlled threshold temperatures in response to a thermal stimulus.
prior_art.relevance: Claims the threshold-calibration methodology for polymer color-change indicators, which is the material-science backbone of how SafeTemp's green-yellow-red calibration would be engineered.

canonical_wren_output.rationale: The most important finding is that the general concept of a thermochromic indicator on a disposable beverage container wall is not novel and is directly claimed in multiple issued patents, so any viable path forward depends entirely on how narrowly and specifically the calibration methodology and material composition claims can be constructed around the physiological burn-risk anchor. I would move immediately to engage a patent attorney or the Pro Bono Program rather than self-filing a provisional, because with this density of blocking prior art the quality of claim drafting is the whole game and a poorly scoped provisional could create more problems than it solves.

next_steps.action: File a provisional application focused exclusively on the narrow defensible claims: the calibration methodology tied to published burn-risk parameters, the three-state color legend system, and the non-toxic food-contact material formulation. Do not file on the broad cup-plus-stri

next_steps.cost_estimate: $5,000-$12,000 for FTO analysis and non-provisional applicat

next_steps.action: Apply through the USPTO Pro Bono Program via a regional partner organization if the founder qualifies as an independent inventor or micro-entity. Given the complex prior art landscape, having a pro bono registered patent practitioner draft and prosecute the application is high-va

queries_used: disposable beverage cup thermochromic temperature indicator strip
method for indicating safe drinking temperature hot liquid container
apparatus thermochromic color change beverage cup burn prevention
single use cup integrated temperature sensitive material food grade

gaps: No located prior art explicitly anchors thermochromic calibration thresholds to a named clinical study or published physiological burn-risk dataset, leaving the calibration-methodology claim potentially open

The combination of a three-state indicator with a printed consumer-readable legend mapping color states to named risk categories on a single-use paper substrate for pediatric caregiving contexts does not appear to be claimed in the located art

Non-toxic medical-grade leuco-dye formulation specifically validated for FDA food-contact compliance on single-use paper cups in direct-beverage-service environments is not addressed in the located art and could support a composition or material claim
patentability.summary: The broad concept of a thermochromic temperature indicator affixed to a disposable beverage container wall is extensively anticipated by prior art dating to at least 2001, and the sleeve-based variant is directly claimed in US8118189B2. As-is, a broad product claim would face serious rejection on anticipation and obviousness grounds. The realistic patentable space lies in narrow, specific claim constructions tied to the physiological burn-risk calibration methodology, the precise multi-state color transition thresholds anchored to the Brown and Diller 54-60 degrees Celsius consumption window, and the combination of medical-grade non-toxic leuco-dye material with single-use food-grade paper substrate in a defined burn-prevention context.
weak_claims: Thermochromic indicator strip affixed to exterior side wall of disposable beverage cup: directly anticipated by US8118189B2 and US20070121702A1 with no meaningful differentiation
Color-change beverage container indicating hot versus safe liquid temperature: anticipated by US20030000451A1 filed 2001, making novelty argument extremely difficult
strong_claims: Calibration method tying discrete thermochromic color state transitions to the 54-60 degrees Celsius physiological burn-risk threshold range derived from documented clinical parameters

Specific leuco-dye or liquid crystal indicator formulation achieving three defined color states on a food-grade paper substrate meeting FDA food-contact and non-toxicity standards for direct-contact d

System combining calibrated multi-state thermochromic indicator with printed temperature-state legend on a single-use paper cup exterior, marketed and designed for pediatric and eldercare burn-prevent

canonical_wren_output.technical_summary: The invention is a disposable paper beverage container incorporating a thermochromic liquid crystal or leuco-dye indicator element affixed to the exterior wall, calibrated to display discrete color states corresponding to defined temperature thresholds within the 54-60 degrees Celsius optimal consumption range. The core claim space sits at the intersection of temperature-responsive material composition, threshold calibration methodology tied to physiological burn-risk parameters, and integration with single-use food-grade substrate construction.

reality_panel.compiled_by: expert curation against public-record patent landscape
reality_panel.notes: Every patent number cited is independently verifiable on Google Patents. Brown and Diller 2008 cited as published academic literature (not blocking patent art). This panel models what a registered patent attorney would deliver after a 4-6 hour formal intake on the SafeTemp brief.

prior_art.title: Temperature-indicating sleeve and related container
prior_art.abstract: An insulating sleeve incorporating a thermochromic indicator visible from the exterior, conveying the temperature of the container contents to the user.
prior_art.relevance: The closest structural anticipation in the consumer-cup space. Directly overlaps the SafeTemp cup-plus-strip form factor.
prior_art.attorney_note: Any broad claim covering 'thermochromic indicator visible on a beverage container exterior' reads directly on this filing and would be rejected on Section 102 anticipation. Workaround paths: claim the cup-and-indicator as a unitary article (not a sleeve), claim the calibration methodology, or claim a specific material composition not disclosed in the 189.

prior_art.title: Beverage container having thermal indicator
prior_art.abstract: A thermochromic thermal indicator affixed to the inner wall of a beverage container, sensing and signaling a specified liquid temperature range.
prior_art.relevance: Oldest blocking reference in the consumer beverage space. Establishes the general concept as 25-year-old prior art.
prior_art.attorney_note: The 2001 priority date establishes that the general thermochromic-on-beverage-container concept has been public prior art for approximately 25 years. Novelty arguments against this filing on the bare concept are not viable.

prior_art.title: Temperature-indicating container
prior_art.abstract: A container with a temperature indicator in or on the side wall or bottom, visible from outside the container.
prior_art.relevance: Sibling filing to Cups Unlimited 189. Forecloses both side-wall and bottom indicator geometries on a disposable cup.
prior_art.attorney_note: The Laguardia claim set reads on both side-wall and bottom indicator positioning, foreclosing the two most common product geometries for a disposable cup. Combined with the 189, this establishes a near-complete blocking set on the SafeTemp form factor as described.

prior_art.title: Temperature-indicating container (PCT)
prior_art.abstract: PCT counterpart to the Laguardia US filing, covering a temperature-indicating container with thermal insulation and side-wall or bottom indicator visible externally.
prior_art.relevance: Extends the Laguardia blocking-art coverage internationally to PCT member states.
prior_art.attorney_note: Together the Laguardia US plus PCT family is the global gating reference for any side-wall thermochromic indicator on a beverage container. International filing is foreclosed without licensing or design-around.

prior_art.title: Advanced multi-element consumable-disposable products
prior_art.abstract: Integration of color-change interactive indicators into high-volume disposable consumable products with minimal incremental manufacturing cost.
prior_art.relevance: Adjacent on the manufacturing-process and multi-element-integration side, not directly anticipating the bare product.
prior_art.attorney_note: Adjacent rather than directly anticipating because Segan emphasizes manufacturing process and multi-element interactions rather than the bare cup-plus-indicator product. Relevant for any production-scale manufacturing or multi-element claim SafeTemp pursues.

prior_art.title: Co-topo polymeric compositions for threshold temperature indication
prior_art.abstract: Polymer-based indicator compositions that undergo reversible or irreversible color change at controlled threshold temperatures.
prior_art.relevance: Covers the polymer chemistry behind calibrated multi-state thermochromic indicators. Material-side blocking potential for any composition claim.
prior_art.attorney_note: If SafeTemp pursues a material composition claim (one of the three defensible angles), Segan's polymer patents must be cleared via formal freedom-to-operate analysis. Likely outcomes: license from Segan, or design around their specific co-topo polymer formulations using alternative leuco-dye or liquid-crystal chemistries.

patentability.summary: The bare product concept of a disposable cup with a thermochromic temperature indicator is anticipated by multiple US and PCT filings dating back to 2001, with Cups Unlimited and the Laguardia family providing dense direct-overlap blocking art. Any broad product claim would face Section 102 anticipation rejection. The defensible patentable space narrows to three specific angles: a calibration methodology claim tied to the published Brown and Diller 2008 burn-threshold dataset; a multi-state color-transition system paired with a printed consumer-readable legend scoped to a specific burn-prevention end-use; and an FDA-compliant non-toxic leuco-dye or liquid-crystal composition claim, subject to formal FTO clearance against the Segan Industries material patents.
strong_claims: Calibration methodology tying discrete thermochromic state transitions to the Brown and Diller 2008 physiological burn-threshold dataset (54-60 Celsius optimal range with 57.8 Celsius preferred-temperature anchor)
Three-state visual-legend system pairing color transitions with a printed consumer-readable safe/caution/hot label, scoped to pediatric or eldercare burn-prevention end-use
FDA-compliant non-toxic leuco-dye composition optimized for direct food-contact on a single-use paper substrate, with stability claims across the 54-60 Celsius calibration range
weak_claims: Generic thermochromic indicator on a disposable beverage cup. Dead against US8118189B2, US20030000451A1, and the Laguardia family. No meaningful differentiation available.
Color-change beverage container indicating hot versus safe liquid temperature. Anticipated by Wilcox 451 filed 2001.
gaps: No located prior art anchors calibration thresholds to a named clinical study or published physiological burn-risk dataset. The calibration-methodology claim path appears open.
Combination of three-state indicator plus printed consumer legend plus pediatric end-use framing is not located in the filed art. Could support a method-of-use or system claim.

FDA-compliant non-toxic leuco-dye composition specifically validated for direct food-contact on single-use paper at the 54-60 Celsius range is not addressed in the located art. Could support a composition claim, subject to Segan FTO clearance.

cpc_codes.label: Containers with thermal insulation or temperature-indicating means
cpc_codes.why: Primary classification. Most blocking prior art lives in this class.

cpc_codes.label: Thermometers using changes in physical or chemical properties of materials, using thermochromic indicators
cpc_codes.why: Covers the indicator element and color-change mechanism. Relevant to calibration and material claims.

cpc_codes.label: Containers made of paper or cardboard, rigid or semi-rigid cups
cpc_codes.why: Substrate classification. Relevant when combining indicator with food-grade paper body claim.

cpc_codes.label: Drinking vessels or lids with means for preventing scalding or burning
cpc_codes.why: Burn-prevention use-case framing. Useful for differentiating on intended physiological safety application.

cpc_codes.label: Liquid crystal compositions for thermochromic applications
cpc_codes.why: Relevant if claims are pursued on the specific liquid crystal or leuco-dye indicator composition.

next_steps.cost_estimate: USPTO filing fee plus -,500 for optional attorney pre-review
next_steps.action: Do not self-file at this prior-art density. Surgical claim drafting on the three defensible angles requires patent-bar training. A poorly scoped provisional creates priority-date confusion and can foreclose better claim language at the non-provisional conversion stage.

next_steps.cost_estimate: ,000-,000 for FTO analysis plus non-provisional drafting and filing
next_steps.action: Retain a registered patent attorney with consumer-goods, polymer-chemistry, or food-contact-materials experience. Scope to formal FTO against US8118189B2, US20030000451A1, the Laguardia US/PCT family, and Segan material patents, then non-provisional claim drafting on the three defensible angles.

next_steps.cost_estimate: Free if income-qualifying. USPTO micro-entity filing fees approximately -.
next_steps.action: Apply via a regional Pro Bono partner (state bar IP sections, law school clinics, LegalCORPS regional affiliates) if the founder meets independent-inventor or micro-entity income criteria. Optimal path for qualifying founders given the complex blocking-art density.

reality_panel.rationale: The thermochromic indicator on disposable beverage containers is heavily filed art with a 25-year prior-art tail, and the consumer-cup form factor is directly claimed in multiple issued patents. The only viable patent strategy is narrow specific claim drafting around the Brown and Diller burn-threshold calibration, the multi-state legend system, and the FDA-compliant material composition, all of which require attorney-grade scoping. Engaging registered counsel through retention or the USPTO Pro Bono Program is the only realistic path to a defensible patent position.

pure_crave.title: Pure Crave Appetite Gum
pure_crave.client_brief: I am developing a natural appetite-suppressant dessert gum called Pure Crave. The product targets late-night cravings and post-meal sweet cravings that derail healthy eating goals. The formulation uses a proprietary gum base blend of chicle, arabic, and candelilla gums, sweetened with xylitol (a sugar alcohol that is tooth-friendly and gentler on blood sugar), with Vitamin E added as an antioxidant. The appetite-management function is delivered through a botanical blend incorporating garcinia cambogia, fenugreek, gymnema sylvestre, and green tea, each used historically for weight management and craving reduction. We are explicit that the use of these botanicals as appetite suppressants has not been clinically proven, so any claims are framed as traditional-use and may-help support, not clinical efficacy. Initial flavors are dessert-themed: cinnamon roll, chocolate mint, gingerbread, and apple variants (classic apple pie, caramel apple, apple cider, apple crumble, green apple). Packaging is compostable or recyclable in trial-size and multi-pack formats. Target market is health-conscious adults managing weight, sugar intake, or specific dietary plans. I want to know if this is patentable and where the prior art sits, particularly around the combination of these specific botanicals in a chicle-base dessert gum delivery format with xylitol sweetener.

cpc_codes.why: This is the primary classification for a chewing gum product where the novelty lies in the active organic botanical compounds in the formulation.
cpc_codes.label: Chewing gum characterised by the composition containing organic or inorganic compounds

cpc_codes.why: Applies directly because xylitol as the primary sweetener and the sugar-free positioning of Pure Crave place it squarely in this subclass.
cpc_codes.label: Chewing gum characterised by the carbohydrate content or the sugar-free nature

cpc_codes.why: Each of the four botanical actives falls under botanical medicinal preparations, and this class will be searched by examiners evaluating the appetite-management utility claim.
cpc_codes.label: Medicinal preparations of undetermined constitution containing material from algae, lichens, fungi, or plants

cpc_codes.why: Covers the pharmaceutical or nutraceutical delivery method angle when chewing gum is used as the vehicle for functional active ingredients like the appetite botanical blend.
cpc_codes.label: Preparations for chewing gum

cpc_codes.why: Relevant if any claim is structured around the multi-region or coated format of the dessert gum product line, including trial-size and multi-pack configurations.
cpc_codes.label: Chewing gum characterised by shape, structure, or form

prior_art.title: Confectionery compositions containing stevia leaves
prior_art.abstract: Discloses confectionery compositions incorporating stevia leaves as a natural sweetener in gum and confectionery products with reduced bitterness and sugar-like taste profiles.
prior_art.relevance: Wrigley holds broad art on natural-sweetener confectionery formulations, and any xylitol-sweetened natural gum claim will be evaluated against this and related Wrigley portfolio filings.

prior_art.title: Natural cooling formulations
prior_art.abstract: Claims natural cooling compound formulations suitable for consumer products including chewing gum, establishing Wrigley art on natural botanical ingredient delivery in gum formats.
prior_art.relevance: Establishes Wrigley prior art on natural botanical actives in gum delivery systems, which is the same delivery channel Pure Crave uses for its botanical appetite blend.

prior_art.title: Cannabinoid chewing gum with sugar alcohols
prior_art.abstract: Claims a chewing gum for mucosal delivery of active compounds using natural resins in the gum base and sugar alcohols as soluble chewing gum ingredients.
prior_art.relevance: Directly anticipates the structural combination of a natural-resin gum base with sugar alcohols as the sweetener system, which maps closely onto the chicle-arabic-candelilla plus xylitol architecture of Pure Crave.

prior_art.title: Cannabinoid chewing gum with high intensity sweeteners
prior_art.abstract: Claims cannabinoid chewing gums using water-insoluble natural-resin gum base combined with sweeteners to mask off-note taste of botanical actives.
prior_art.relevance: The method of using a natural gum base to deliver a botanical active with sweetener masking is directly analogous to Pure Crave's delivery mechanism, even though the active is different.

prior_art.title: Coated multi-region confectionery and method for producing the same
prior_art.abstract: Claims a multi-region confectionery product incorporating a chewing gum region and a candy region within a particulate coated structure.
prior_art.relevance: Background art on confectionery-gum hybrid formats that could be cited against any dessert-flavored multi-region format claim for Pure Crave.

prior_art.title: Sugar free confectionery; methods of making same; and use in preparing multilayer structures
prior_art.abstract: Discloses sugar-free confectionery products and methods of making multilayer structures combining confectionery and chewing gum components.
prior_art.relevance: Broad Intercontinental Great Brands art on sugar-free gum formulations establishes a crowded field for any unqualified sugar-free or xylitol gum composition claim.

canonical_wren_output.rationale: The most important finding is that no retrieved patent directly claims the specific combination of a chicle-arabic-candelilla three-component natural gum base co-formulated with all four of these botanicals in a single chewing gum for appetite management, which means there is a real but narrow composition-of-matter window if claims are drafted precisely around that combination with defined ratios. I would push the founder toward either the USPTO Pro Bono Program or a patent attorney before filing anything, because the Wrigley and Intercontinental Great Brands portfolios are broad enough that a loosely drafted provisional could create a false sense of protection while leaving the actual defensible claim angles underdeveloped.

next_steps.action: File a provisional patent application describing the specific three-component gum base, the four-botanical combination with approximate weight ratios, and the xylitol sweetener system to lock a priority date while you validate market demand over the next 12 months before committi
next_steps.cost_estimate: $320 USPTO filing fee for a micro-entity plus drafting time

next_steps.action: Retain a patent attorney with food science or nutraceutical formulation experience to conduct a formal freedom-to-operate analysis against the Wrigley and Intercontinental Great Brands portfolios, then draft claims tightly around the specific botanical combination and multi-compo
next_steps.cost_estimate: $5,000-$12,000 for FTO plus provisional or non-provisional d

next_steps.action: Apply through the USPTO Pro Bono Program, which matches under-resourced independent inventors with volunteer patent attorneys; given the complexity of the crowded botanical-gum space and the need for precise claim drafting, free professional help is worth pursuing before spending
next_steps.cost_estimate: Free if qualifying under income and entity-size criteria

queries_used: chewing gum composition botanical appetite suppressant xylitol
method delivering garcinia cambogia gymnema sylvestre gum base
chicle arabic candelilla gum blend weight management formulation
apparatus system encapsulating botanical extract chewing gum confection

gaps: The precise three-component chicle-arabic-candelilla gum base as a defined structural matrix does not appear claimed in any retrieved result, representing a potential composition gap

The specific four-botanical synergistic combination at defined ratios within that natural gum base for craving suppression targeting a post-meal or late-night use case is absent from the retrieved prior art
Dessert-flavor-profile-matched botanical gum systems with traditional-use appetite framing and sustainable packaging as a combined product claim appear entirely unclaimed in the results
patentability.summary: The general concept of a botanical-functional chewing gum sweetened with sugar alcohols is well-trodden territory, as evidenced by the natural-resin plus sugar-alcohol gum base architecture already claimed in the cannabinoid gum space and Wrigley's broad natural-ingredient confectionery portfolio. However, the specific four-botanical combination of garcinia cambogia, fenugreek, gymnema sylvestre, and green tea extract co-formulated in a three-component natural gum base of chicle, gum arabic, and candelilla does not appear to be directly claimed in the retrieved results, leaving a potential composition-of-matter window for that precise combination. Patentability as filed would likely require narrowing claims tightly to that specific multi-botanical plus multi-gum-base combination, and the appetite-management utility framing would need careful drafting to avoid running into obviousness rejections given the known individual properties of each botanical.
weak_claims: Any broad claim to a botanical appetite-suppressant chewing gum sweetened with xylitol, without specific gum base combination or botanical ratios, is likely DOA given the natural-resin plus sugar-alcohol prior art in CA3031530C and the Wrigley natural-ingredient portfolio
Gymnema sylvestre or garcinia cambogia alone in a gum base as an appetite aid is almost certainly anticipated by pre-existing supplement and confectionery literature outside the retrieved patents
strong_claims: Composition-of-matter claim for the specific combination of chicle, gum arabic, and candelilla as a three-component natural gum base matrix co-formulated with all four named botanicals in a single che
Method of delivering a synergistic botanical craving-suppression blend via a xylitol-sweetened natural multi-resin gum base with defined weight ratios of each botanical active
Flavored chewing gum composition combining the four-botanical appetite blend with dessert-flavor profiles and a compostable packaging system as a claimed product system

canonical_wren_output.technical_summary: The core technical claim is a chewing gum composition comprising a multi-component natural gum base of chicle, gum arabic, and candelilla wax, sweetened with xylitol, and functionalized with a botanical blend of garcinia cambogia, fenugreek, gymnema sylvestre, and green tea extract for appetite modulation and craving suppression. I searched across the apparatus angle for the gum base matrix itself, the method angle for botanical delivery via chewing gum, the combination angle for the specific multi-gum-base formulation, and the use-case angle for weight management and satiety applications in confectionery delivery systems.

reality_panel.compiled_by: expert curation against public-record patent landscape
reality_panel.notes: Same six patents Wren independently surfaced. Each is publicly verifiable on Google Patents by publication number. This panel models what a registered patent attorney with food-science or nutraceutical-formulation experience would deliver after a formal 4-6 hour intake on the Pure Crave brief.

prior_art.title: Cannabinoid chewing gum with sugar alcohols
prior_art.abstract: A chewing gum for mucosal delivery of active compounds using natural resins in the gum base and sugar alcohols as the sweetener system.
prior_art.relevance: Closest structural anticipation in the natural-resin gum base plus sugar alcohol delivery space.
prior_art.attorney_note: Medcan's claims do not name the specific resin combination of chicle, arabic, and candelilla, but they cover the general architecture of a natural-resin matrix plus sugar alcohol. A broad Pure Crave claim covering natural-resin gum sweetened with xylitol delivering a botanical active reads directly on this filing. Workaround paths: claim the specific three-component resin combination, claim the specific botanical quartet, or claim defined weight ratios across the multi-component composition.

prior_art.title: Cannabinoid chewing gum with high intensity sweeteners
prior_art.abstract: Cannabinoid chewing gums using water-insoluble natural-resin gum base combined with sweeteners to mask off-note taste of botanical actives.
prior_art.relevance: Methodologically analogous delivery framework. Different active class but same vehicle architecture.
prior_art.attorney_note: Adjacent because Nordiccan's claims center on cannabinoid actives plus taste-masking, not appetite suppression. The natural-resin-as-delivery-vehicle-for-botanical-active framework reads on Pure Crave's mechanism even though the active class differs. Examiner will likely cite this for any broad delivery-method claim.

prior_art.title: Confectionery compositions containing stevia leaves
prior_art.abstract: Confectionery compositions using stevia leaves as a natural sweetener in gum and confectionery with reduced bitterness.
prior_art.relevance: Member of the broad Wrigley natural-sweetener confectionery portfolio. Cited as representative of the crowded field.
prior_art.attorney_note: Wrigley's natural-sweetener confectionery portfolio is broad and active. Any xylitol-sweetened natural gum claim will be evaluated against the full Wrigley filing family, not just this single reference. FTO scope must include all active Wrigley natural-sweetener and natural-ingredient gum filings.

prior_art.title: Natural cooling formulations
prior_art.abstract: Natural cooling compound formulations for consumer products including chewing gum.
prior_art.relevance: Establishes Wrigley prior art on natural botanical active delivery via gum. Same delivery channel as Pure Crave.
prior_art.attorney_note: High-impact for examiner obviousness analysis. Pure Crave's appetite-management delivery uses the same channel type (botanical active in gum). The Wrigley portfolio collectively constitutes a crowded-field problem for any natural-ingredient gum claim.

prior_art.title: Sugar free confectionery; methods of making same; and use in preparing multilayer structures
prior_art.abstract: Sugar-free confectionery products including multilayer structures combining confectionery and chewing gum.
prior_art.relevance: Establishes the crowded sugar-free gum field. Forecloses any generic sugar-free xylitol gum claim.
prior_art.attorney_note: A generic sugar-free xylitol gum claim is foreclosed by this and related Intercontinental Great Brands filings. Pure Crave's specific multi-resin base plus four-botanical claim avoids this directly. Reference will appear in any examiner search but is non-blocking on the specific defensible composition.

prior_art.title: Coated multi-region confectionery and method for producing the same
prior_art.abstract: Multi-region confectionery products incorporating a chewing gum region and a candy region in a coated structure.
prior_art.relevance: Background art on confectionery-gum hybrid formats. Relevant only if Pure Crave pursues coated or multi-region product extensions.
prior_art.attorney_note: Non-blocking on the bare gum composition claim. Relevant only if Pure Crave pursues a multi-region or coated-format claim such as a botanical-loaded core with a dessert-flavor coating. Worth flagging for any future product-line extension that adds coated or layered formats.

patentability.summary: The general concept of a botanical-functional chewing gum sweetened with sugar alcohols is well-trodden art (CA3031530C, US12274782B2, the Wrigley natural-ingredient family). Pure Crave's specific four-botanical combination of garcinia cambogia, fenugreek, gymnema sylvestre, and green tea co-formulated in the three-component chicle, arabic, and candelilla resin matrix with defined weight ratios does not appear in the retrieved art. A narrow composition-of-matter claim around that precise combination is the defensible path. Broad utility framing for appetite management will face Section 103 obviousness analysis because each individual botanical has known traditional-use literature for appetite or craving reduction. The defensible utility framing avoids efficacy claims and instead asserts traditional-use support plus the novel multi-component synergy.
strong_claims: Composition-of-matter claim for the specific chicle plus arabic plus candelilla three-component natural gum base co-formulated with all four named botanicals (garcinia cambogia, fenugreek, gymnema sylvestre, green tea) with defined weight-ratio ranges
Method-of-delivery claim for the four-botanical blend via a xylitol-sweetened natural multi-resin gum base targeting post-meal or late-night use cases
Product-system claim combining the four-botanical appetite blend with dessert-flavor profiles and a compostable packaging system
weak_claims: Generic claim to a botanical appetite-suppressant chewing gum sweetened with xylitol. Anticipated by the natural-resin-plus-sugar-alcohol architecture in CA3031530C and the Wrigley natural-ingredient portfolio.
Any individual botanical (gymnema alone, garcinia alone, fenugreek alone) in a gum base. Anticipated by extensive supplement and confectionery literature outside the located patent results.
gaps: The precise three-component chicle plus arabic plus candelilla gum base as a defined structural matrix does not appear claimed in retrieved results.
The specific four-botanical combination at defined ratios within a natural gum base for craving suppression is not claimed in retrieved results.
Dessert-flavor-profile-matched botanical gum systems with traditional-use appetite framing and sustainable packaging as a combined product claim are not addressed in the retrieved art.

cpc_codes.label: Chewing gum characterised by composition containing organic or inorganic compounds
cpc_codes.why: Primary classification. The novelty lives in the botanical organic actives within the formulation.

cpc_codes.label: Chewing gum characterised by carbohydrate content or sugar-free nature
cpc_codes.why: Applies because xylitol is the primary sweetener and Pure Crave positions as sugar-free.

cpc_codes.label: Medicinal preparations of undetermined constitution containing material from algae, lichens, fungi, or plants
cpc_codes.why: Each of the four botanicals falls under this class. Examiner search will hit here for the appetite-management utility framing.

cpc_codes.label: Preparations for chewing gum (pharmaceutical or nutraceutical delivery)
cpc_codes.why: Covers the gum-as-vehicle delivery method angle when chewing gum delivers functional active ingredients.

cpc_codes.label: Chewing gum characterised by shape, structure, or form
cpc_codes.why: Relevant for any multi-region or coated product line extension.

next_steps.cost_estimate: USPTO filing fee for micro-entity plus drafting time
next_steps.action: A precision-drafted provisional locking in the specific gum base composition, the four-botanical blend with weight ratios, and the xylitol delivery system can establish a priority date. Caveat: the value depends entirely on how precisely the specification is written. A vague provisional locks in a vague priority date. Recommended only if drafted with attorney pre-review.

next_steps.cost_estimate: ,000-,000 for FTO analysis plus provisional or non-provisional drafting
next_steps.action: Retain a patent attorney with food science or nutraceutical formulation experience. Scope work to formal FTO against the full Wrigley natural-ingredient gum portfolio, the Intercontinental Great Brands sugar-free gum family, and the Medcan/Nordiccan cannabinoid-gum filings, followed by claim drafting tightly around the four-botanical plus three-resin composition with defined ratios.

next_steps.cost_estimate: Free if income-qualifying. USPTO micro-entity filing fees approximately -.
next_steps.action: Apply via a regional Pro Bono partner (state bar IP sections, law school clinics, LegalCORPS regional affiliates) if the founder meets independent-inventor or micro-entity criteria. Given the crowded botanical-gum field, free attorney involvement is high-value before any provisional gets filed.

reality_panel.rationale: The botanical-functional gum space is well-trodden territory dominated by the Wrigley, Intercontinental Great Brands, and Medcan/Nordiccan filing families, but the specific four-botanical combination in a chicle plus arabic plus candelilla three-component matrix with defined weight ratios appears genuinely unclaimed. The defensible patent strategy is narrow composition-of-matter drafting around that precise combination, with appetite-management utility framed as traditional-use rather than clinical efficacy. Engaging counsel through retention or the USPTO Pro Bono Program is the only realistic path to a defensible position.

second_chance.title: Second Chance Fitness App
second_chance.client_brief: I am developing a fitness tracking app called Second Chance Fitness that introduces a more forgiving exercise adherence model than current trackers. The core innovation is a 7-day sliding grace window: when a user does not complete their daily fitness goal (exercise minutes, steps, or a custom metric), the unfilled daily circle remains active for up to seven days, during which an additional workout session can be applied retroactively to close the missed day. The visual representation uses circular daily-goal indicators similar to closed-ring concepts but with the extended grace window. The app also tracks bounceback streaks that celebrate consistency after a missed day rather than penalizing the break, supports user-customizable target values for exercise minutes or step counts, and integrates with popular wearables and health platforms via standard fitness APIs. The target user is busy professionals and parents with unpredictable schedules, individuals prone to all-or-nothing thinking around exercise, and beginners intimidated by rigid daily tracking. The revenue model is freemium with paid in-app challenge subscriptions. I want to know if this is patentable and where the prior art sits, particularly around the rolling 7-day adherence mechanism, the bounceback-streak interaction model, and whether software UI claims of this shape survive Section 101 abstract-idea analysis.

cpc_codes.why: Covers computer-implemented exercise monitoring and goal-tracking systems, the primary classification home for the brief's activity adherence method.
cpc_codes.label: Exercise apparatus with electronic timing or scoring

cpc_codes.why: Directly applies to software systems that monitor whether users are adhering to health or activity plans, which is the functional core of the grace-window mechanism.
cpc_codes.label: ICT specially adapted for therapies or health-improving plans, e.g. for handling prescriptions, for steering therapy or for monitoring patient compliance

cpc_codes.why: Relevant to the circular pending-state visual indicators and any UI-layer claims the applicant chooses to pursue alongside the method claims.
cpc_codes.label: Interaction techniques for graphical user interfaces based on specific properties of the displayed elements

cpc_codes.why: Applies to the business-method and system architecture layer of a health and wellness software platform, supporting broader system claims.
cpc_codes.label: Health care, e.g. insurance, care of the aged

cpc_codes.why: Covers wearable sensor data acquisition and physiological metric ingestion, relevant to the external fitness API integration component of the brief.
cpc_codes.label: Measuring for diagnostic purposes; identification of persons

prior_art.title: Displaying user interfaces associated with physical activities
prior_art.abstract: Discloses user interfaces for displaying physical activity metrics including circular activity ring indicators, activity competitions, and social fitness tracking features on wearable and mobile devices.
prior_art.relevance: Apple's closed-ring activity visualization is the closest visual and structural analog to the circular daily-goal indicators described in the brief, making this the most important defensive reference for UI claims.

prior_art.title: DISPLAY OF USER INTERFACES ASSOCIATED WITH PHYSICAL ACTIVITIES
prior_art.abstract: Covers techniques for displaying circular activity ring user interfaces associated with physical activity goals, competitions, and friend-list social features on Apple devices.
prior_art.relevance: This earlier Apple family member strengthens the prior-art wall around circular daily-goal ring UI and streak-based activity display, directly informing what visual claim scope is available to a new filer.

prior_art.title: Communication and user interface control in user activity monitoring systems
prior_art.abstract: Discloses a non-transitory computer-readable medium with an application that controls communication and user interface elements in user activity monitoring systems, including processing activity data inputs from electronic devices.
prior_art.assignee: T.J. Hooker and Modifier Limited Company
prior_art.relevance: This reference covers user-interface control logic tied to activity monitoring, which could read on the brief's configurable threshold parameters and API-ingestion pipeline if claims are drafted broadly.

prior_art.title: Automated health data acquisition, processing and communication system
prior_art.abstract: Discloses a system that receives health and extrinsic data, computes a composite Health Score via algorithmic processing, and communicates results to users through a connected platform.
prior_art.relevance: The Dacadoo system's multi-parameter health scoring and data-ingestion architecture is background art for the brief's wearable API integration and configurable metric aggregation layer.

prior_art.title: System and method for monitoring safety and productivity of physical tasks
prior_art.abstract: Discloses methods and systems that receive wearable sensor signals, identify physical activity portions, and evaluate task performance against safety and productivity thresholds.
prior_art.relevance: This reference establishes prior art for receiving wearable-device activity signals and comparing them against configurable thresholds, which overlaps with the brief's external fitness API ingestion and user-configurable target parameters.

prior_art.title: Dynamic Resource Location Coordination Control System
prior_art.abstract: Discloses a location-centric system that controls dispatch of primary and secondary tasks using vectors with both time and space domains, coordinating mobile resources across a network.
prior_art.relevance: The time-domain task sequencing and secondary-task substitution logic is a distant structural analog to the brief's grace-window retroactive goal-satisfaction mechanism, worth monitoring during prosecution.

canonical_wren_output.rationale: The most important finding is that the circular ring UI territory is densely held by Apple's active patent family, so the real patentable value here lives entirely in the time-windowed retroactive satisfaction algorithm and the grace-recovery streak logic, neither of which appears anywhere in the retrieved results. I would engage a software patent attorney before filing anything, because the Section 101 abstract-idea question on a method like this lives or dies on how precisely the claims are drafted around the concrete data-processing steps, and that is exactly where a provisional self-filed in a hurry tends to leave gaps that are almost impossible to close later.

next_steps.action: Draft a provisional application focused exclusively on the retroactive session-attribution method and the grace-recovery streak algorithm using the technical summary as the specification backbone, file within 12 months to establish a priority date, then use that year to validate
next_steps.cost_estimate: $320 USPTO filing fee plus time investment

next_steps.action: Retain a software patent attorney with Section 101 experience to draft independent claims tightly anchored to the concrete computational steps of the time-windowed pending-state mechanism, conduct a formal freedom-to-operate analysis against Apple's ring-UI family, and prepare a

Apply through a regional USPTO Pro Bono coordinator if the founder qualifies as an independent inventor with limited resources, providing the technical summary and this scout report to the matched attorney to accelerate the engagement.

queries_used: method for retroactive goal completion sliding time window fitness tracking
system for exercise adherence recovery grace period activity monitoring
apparatus for bounceback streak calculation missed workout session recovery
method for rolling seven day activity goal assignment wearable device

gaps: No retrieved patent addresses the specific retroactive session-attribution algorithm in which a later workout is computationally linked to a prior pending goal node across a sliding multi-day window
The bounceback-streak model that triggers a positive counter increment on grace-recovery rather than streak termination is absent from all retrieved results and represents genuine white space
Configurable per-user grace-window duration combined with dynamic goal-node state management across heterogeneous fitness API sources does not appear in any retrieved reference
patentability.summary: The circular-ring UI and streak concepts face a real headwind from Apple's active patent family, which dominates that visual territory, so UI-only claims drafted without a concrete technical differentiator are high risk under Section 101 abstract-idea doctrine. However, the specific combination of a configurable multi-day pending-state node, retroactive session attribution logic, and a positive-reinforcement streak counter that resets on grace-period recovery rather than terminating on a missed event does not appear in the retrieved results and represents a plausible narrow method claim. The strongest path is to anchor claims to the concrete data-processing steps of the time-windowed retroactive satisfaction algorithm rather than the visual presentation layer.
weak_claims: Circular visual indicator for daily activity goals: directly adjacent to Apple's active ring-UI patent family and vulnerable to Section 101 challenge as an abstract idea without additional structural recitation
Generic wearable API integration for step and exercise-minute ingestion: well-established in prior art including Dacadoo and One Million Metrics references, offering little differentiation
strong_claims: Method for retroactively closing a pending daily activity goal node by attributing a subsequent qualifying session to the node within a configurable multi-day grace window
Streak-continuity calculation apparatus that increments a positive-reinforcement counter upon successful grace-period recovery rather than resetting it on the missed-day event
System for maintaining a multi-state daily goal record with a time-bounded pending state that persists across calendar days until a configurable expiry threshold is reached

canonical_wren_output.technical_summary: The core claims architecture centers on a time-windowed retroactive goal-satisfaction method in which an unfulfilled daily activity metric remains in a pending state for a configurable multi-day grace period, during which a subsequent qualifying activity session can be applied to close the prior incomplete goal node. Secondary claims involve a streak-continuity calculation apparatus that resets a positive-reinforcement counter upon successful grace-period recovery rather than terminating the streak on the missed-day event, integrated with user-configurable threshold parameters and external fitness API data ingestion.

reality_panel.compiled_by: expert curation against public-record patent landscape
reality_panel.notes: Same six patents Wren independently surfaced. This case is dominated by Section 101 abstract-idea risk under the post-Alice software-claim framework, alongside Apple's active ring-UI patent family. The defensible patent strategy lives at the algorithmic data-processing layer, not the visual presentation layer.

prior_art.title: Displaying user interfaces associated with physical activities
prior_art.abstract: User interfaces for displaying physical activity metrics including circular activity ring indicators, activity competitions, and social fitness tracking features on wearable and mobile devices.
prior_art.relevance: Apple's closed-ring activity visualization. Dominant prior art for circular daily-goal indicators.
prior_art.attorney_note: Combined with the US, EU, and CN counterparts, this Australian family member locks down the bare visual ring-UI territory globally. Any Second Chance claim drafted at the visual-presentation layer reads on this family. The defensive value of pursuing visual-layer claims is near zero.

prior_art.title: Display of user interfaces associated with physical activities
prior_art.abstract: Techniques for displaying circular activity ring user interfaces associated with physical activity goals, competitions, and friend-list social features on Apple devices.
prior_art.relevance: Earlier-priority member of the Apple ring-UI family. Establishes the 2018 priority date for the broader portfolio.
prior_art.attorney_note: Reinforces that visual-presentation territory is densely held with multi-jurisdictional coverage. Second Chance must avoid this entire family on the UI side and instead claim the algorithmic layer below it.

prior_art.title: Communication and user interface control in user activity monitoring systems
prior_art.assignee: T.J. Hooker and Modifier Limited Company
prior_art.abstract: Non-transitory computer-readable medium controlling UI elements in activity monitoring systems, processing activity data from electronic devices.
prior_art.relevance: Covers data-pipeline-plus-UI-control intersection. Could read on Second Chance's wearable API ingestion plus configurable threshold logic if claims are drafted broadly.
prior_art.attorney_note: Narrow claim drafting can avoid this reference. The risk is broad coverage on the API ingestion plus threshold comparison architecture. Independent claims must recite the specific grace-window mechanic to distinguish.

prior_art.title: Automated health data acquisition, processing and communication system
prior_art.abstract: Receiving health and extrinsic data, computing a composite Health Score, communicating results through a connected platform.
prior_art.relevance: Background art on multi-parameter health scoring and data ingestion architecture.
prior_art.attorney_note: The composite-scoring layer is mechanically similar to Second Chance's metric-aggregation layer. Background only. Not blocking on the specific grace-window retroactive-attribution mechanism.

prior_art.title: System and method for monitoring safety and productivity of physical tasks
prior_art.abstract: Methods and systems that receive wearable sensor signals, identify physical activity portions, and evaluate task performance against configurable thresholds.
prior_art.relevance: Background reference for wearable-signal ingestion plus configurable threshold comparison.
prior_art.attorney_note: Overlap is methodological rather than claim-blocking. Notable for the configurable threshold concept it teaches as prior art. Independent claims must distinguish on the specific grace-window plus retroactive-attribution mechanic.

prior_art.title: Dynamic Resource Location Coordination Control System
prior_art.abstract: Location-centric system controlling dispatch of primary and secondary tasks using vectors with both time and space domains.
prior_art.relevance: Distant structural analog. Time-domain task sequencing plus secondary-task substitution logic loosely maps to retroactive goal-satisfaction within a time window.
prior_art.attorney_note: Distant enough that it does not directly block but worth flagging during prosecution. The kind of reference an examiner could cite for the time-windowed substitution concept. Mention in any specification background section as a recognized but distant analog.

patentability.summary: This case presents a Section 101 abstract-idea problem alongside the Section 102 anticipation analysis. Apple's active ring-UI patent family (AU2020204153B2, DK179992B1, and counterparts) dominates the visual-presentation layer, foreclosing any UI-only claim drafted at the ring or streak visualization level. Independent of the Apple family, software-method claims of this shape face Section 101 abstract-idea rejection under Alice unless they recite concrete data-processing steps with technical specificity. The defensible patent strategy is to abandon visual-layer claims entirely and anchor independent claims to the concrete computational mechanics of the time-windowed retroactive session-attribution algorithm and the bounceback-streak grace-recovery logic. Both subject-matter areas appear absent from the retrieved art.
strong_claims: Method for retroactively closing a pending daily activity goal node by attributing a subsequent qualifying session within a configurable multi-day grace window, with the algorithm specified at the data-structure and operation level

Streak-continuity calculation apparatus that increments a positive-reinforcement counter on successful grace-period recovery rather than terminating the streak on the missed-day event, claimed as a specific computational procedure

System for maintaining a multi-state daily goal record with a time-bounded pending state persisting across calendar days, claimed with concrete reference to the data structures and state-transition operations
weak_claims: Circular visual indicator for daily activity goals, with or without streaks. Section 101 abstract-idea vulnerability plus direct conflict with the Apple ring-UI family. Avoid.
Generic wearable API integration for step or exercise-minute ingestion. Well-established prior art (Dacadoo, One Million Metrics, broad mHealth literature). Differentiation is minimal.
gaps: The specific retroactive session-attribution algorithm linking a later workout computationally to a prior pending goal node across a sliding multi-day window is not addressed in retrieved art.
The bounceback-streak model that increments a positive counter on grace-recovery rather than terminating the streak is absent from all retrieved results.
Configurable per-user grace-window duration combined with dynamic goal-node state management across heterogeneous fitness API sources does not appear in retrieved art.

cpc_codes.label: Exercise apparatus with electronic timing or scoring
cpc_codes.why: Primary classification for computer-implemented exercise monitoring and goal-tracking.

cpc_codes.label: ICT specially adapted for therapies or health-improving plans, monitoring patient compliance
cpc_codes.why: Directly applies to software systems monitoring user adherence to activity plans. Core class for the grace-window mechanism.

cpc_codes.label: Interaction techniques for graphical user interfaces based on displayed elements
cpc_codes.why: Relevant to circular pending-state visual indicators. Lower priority given the recommendation to avoid UI claims.

cpc_codes.label: Health care, e.g. insurance, care of the aged
cpc_codes.why: Applies to business-method and system architecture layer of a health and wellness platform.

cpc_codes.label: Measuring for diagnostic purposes; identification of persons
cpc_codes.why: Covers wearable sensor data acquisition and physiological metric ingestion.

next_steps.cost_estimate: USPTO filing fee plus drafting time
next_steps.action: Not recommended without attorney drafting assistance. Section 101 abstract-idea claims fail or succeed on the precise wording of the independent claim. A self-drafted provisional almost certainly drops to the abstract-idea layer rather than the concrete-data-processing layer, locking in a priority date for unpatentable claim language.

next_steps.cost_estimate: ,000-,000 for FTO plus Section 101-aware provisional drafting
next_steps.action: Retain a software patent attorney with documented Section 101 experience. The right attorney profile has post-Alice prosecution history showing successful claim drafting around abstract-idea rejections. Scope work to formal FTO against the Apple ring-UI family across multiple jurisdictions, plus claim drafting tightly anchored to the concrete computational steps of the time-windowed pending-state mechanism and grace-recovery streak algorithm.

Apply if qualifying, but recognize that the Section 101 expertise required is specialized and Pro Bono matching may not produce an attorney with the right software-prosecution background. Vet the matched practitioner specifically for post-Alice software-method experience before proceeding.

reality_panel.rationale: The visual ring-UI territory is densely held by Apple's active patent family across multiple jurisdictions and is non-patentable terrain for any new entrant. The genuine patentable space is the time-windowed retroactive session-attribution algorithm and the bounceback-streak grace-recovery logic, both of which appear unclaimed in the retrieved art. Section 101 abstract-idea risk is the dominant patentability constraint and demands a software patent attorney with documented post-Alice experience.
